sections.paragraph: a primitive word, in sing. not used, signifying a man, vir, hence homo, man in general. The Hebrews used for it the contracted and softer form אִישׁ (comp. Gr. εἱς for ἑνς, gen. ἑνος), a man, vir, also the prolonged form אֱנֹושׁ homo. From the primary form is the fem. אִשָּׁה for אִנְשָׁה, and pl. אֲנָשִׁים. The signification of sickness and disease which is found in the root אָנַשׁ, is taken from another source (from the theme נשׁ) altogether foreign to this noun as a root.
